{{Quote|I go where I'm needed.|Jon Antilles|<ref name="Jon" />}}
'''Jon Antilles''' was the name—though many believed it was an alias—of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who lived during the outbreak of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the conflict between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. [[Jedi training/Legends|Trained]] by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master known as the [[The Dark Woman|Dark Woman]], who was known for her unusual and often harsh training methods, Antilles followed in his Master's path and roamed [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], letting [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] be his guide. 

Considered as a legend among his fellow Jedi, one of Antilles's many accomplishments was infiltrating the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]] and bringing known murderers to justice. Because of his nomadic nature and tendency to go on long periods of [[Time/Legends|time]] without reporting back to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], Antilles was thought to be [[Death/Legends|dead]] many times over.

In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], four [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], Antilles went to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Queyta]] where a Separatist [[Separatist Queyta base|base]] was located. Along with four Jedi Masters—[[Knol Ven'nari]], [[Nico Diath]], [[Fay]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]—Antilles was [[Mission to Queyta|tasked]] with acquiring the antidote for [[swamp gas]], a [[Bioweapon/Legends|chemical weapon]] that the Separatists planned to use against the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. 

Unbeknownst to the Jedi, the base was actually a trap laid out by Separatist [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] and the [[Gen'Dai/Legends|Gen'Dai]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Durge/Legends|Durge]]. Antilles confronted Durge, allowing Kenobi and Fay to pursue Ventress and retrieve the antidote from her. Proving to be stronger, Durge killed Antilles by grabbing the Jedi and physically submerging him into one of the many [[Magma/Legends|lava]] streams that flowed through the base.

==Biography==
===Early years===
{{Quote|Jon Antilles…though, we doubt that was his ''real'' name…A student of the Dark Woman, who years ago infiltrated the Bounty Hunter's Guild and brought many murderers to justice…|Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="Jon" />}}
[[File:DarkWoman-SWREP13.jpg|thumb|right|Jon Antilles was the Padawan of The Dark Woman (pictured).]]
Jon Antilles was the name of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="DB" /> [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who lived during the beginning of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a major [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], though many of his fellow [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] believed it was not his real name but merely an alias. At some point in Antilles's early life, he became a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of the [[The Dark Woman|Dark Woman]],<ref name="Jon" /> a Jedi Master who was known to have subjected her Padawans to unorthodox [[Jedi training/Legends|training methods]].<ref name="SWRE">[[Star Wars (1998) 13|''Star Wars'' (1998) 13]]</ref>

After his apprenticeship, Antilles roamed the galaxy and allowed [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to be his guide. He was considered a nomad by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], refusing to take a Padawan learner of his own. He often went through long periods of [[Time/Legends|time]] without reporting back to the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], leading to many instances of him being believed [[Death/Legends|dead]]. Despite his nomadic lifestyle, Antilles's substantial achievements, which included infiltrating the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]] and bringing many murderers to justice, spoke volumes to his mastery of the Force.<ref name="Jon" />

===Mission to Queyta===
====Assembling the team====
{{Quote|Everyone land safely? Let's find a way in.|Jon Antilles, to the strike team|<ref name="Jon" />}}
[[File:Queyta.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Antilles took part in a mission to Queyta]]
In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], four [[Standard month/Legends|months]] into the Clone Wars, Antilles went to the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Queyta]] to [[Mission to Queyta|assist]] four of his fellow Jedi—Masters [[Knol Ven'nari]], [[Nico Diath]], [[Fay]], and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]—in finding the antidote for a Separatist [[Bioweapon/Legends|chemical weapon]] called [[swamp gas]], a lethal airborne gas that was first tested on [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] colonists on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Ohma-D'un/Legends|Ohma-D'un]] and resulted in great casualties among the colonists. After the successful initial test, the Separatists planned to use the chemical weapon against the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]].<ref name="Jon" />

Even though the Jedi High Council had not assigned him to the strike team, Antilles followed the [[Living Force/Legends|will of the Force]] and joined the group. Following their infiltration of the mobile Separatist [[Separatist Queyta base|base]], which was located on a small island moving through the planet's [[Magma/Legends|lava]] stream, the group learned that Separatist [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] and the [[Gen'Dai/Legends|Gen'Dai]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Durge/Legends|Durge]] were waiting for them. Informed by their [[Spy/Legends|spies]], Ventress and Durge had known of the Jedi strike team's arrival in advance and had prepared for the assault.<ref name="Jon" />

====Engaging the Separatists====
{{Quote|Obi-Wan! Don't let the woman escape! I'll hold this monster back.|Jon Antilles, to Obi-Wan Kenobi|<ref name="Jon" />}}
Antilles [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|engaged]] Ventress in a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|lightsaber duel]] before being forced off a narrow walkway by Durge. With the antidote in her hands, Ventress tried to escape with Fay and Kenobi chasing after her. Durge blocked the pair's path, but Antilles took it upon himself to distract the [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]], allowing his companions to continue pursuing Ventress. In his final moments, Antilles resorted to physical confrontation and grappled with Durge, grabbing him from behind. Although a skilled Jedi, Antilles was no match for the Gen'Dai bounty hunter; Durge incinerated Antilles by pressing him into a lava stream.<ref name="Jon" />

[[File:Queyta2.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The monuments created for the slain Jedi Masters]]
Kenobi, the only survivor of the Jedi strike team, was successful in retrieving the antidote. He managed to return to the Jedi Temple on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] despite his many injuries sustained from the mission, and handed the antidote over to Jedi High Council member [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] for mass production. After being treated for his wounds, Kenobi joined his fellow Jedi in [[Funeral/Legends|honoring]] the four deceased Masters for their sacrifice.<ref name="Jon" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Antilles, Master Diath is in charge here. You weren't even assigned to this mission, drifter.|Knol Ven'nari|<ref name="Jon" />}}
Despite his tutelage under the Dark Woman, considered by many as someone who often tread too close to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]],<ref name="SWRE" /> Antilles was a firm believer of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]]. While he was engaged in a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] duel with Ventress, Antilles expressed his opinion that Ventress was already destroyed by falling to the dark side.<ref name="Jon" />

Antilles followed the Living Force, traveling across the galaxy and delivering justice when needed, as shown when he apprehended several murderers within the Bounty Hunters' Guild. He allowed the Living Force to guide his decisions and actions, even to the point of ignoring official orders from the Jedi High Council. One such example was when he joined the Jedi strike team on Queyta just by following the Living Force, despite not receiving any official briefing for the mission. When Master Knol Ven'nari berated him for not being assigned to the strike team, Antilles merely replied that he was needed.<ref name="Jon" />

During the mission to Queyta, Antilles exhibited signs of being a disobedient loner, barely acknowledging Nico Diath's authority and taking the lead during the team's infiltration of the Separatist base. Despite this, he was cautious and observant, advising the team that no path was completely safe after discovering no [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] were deployed. When Master Knol Ven'nari suggested a direct assault on the [[Skakoan/Legends|Skakoan]] guards, Antilles pointed out that their pressurized suits would explode if pierced by lightsabers. His bravery and selflessness were evident in his final act, facing Durge alone and sacrificing his life to give his companions a chance to complete the mission.<ref name="Jon" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|He was believed dead many times before, but this time, I don't think he's coming back.|Obi-Wan Kenobi, on Jon Antilles|<ref name="Jon" />}}
[[File:JonAntilles-SWR53.jpg|thumb|right|280px|Jon Antilles using Force flight on Queyta]]
As a Padawan of the Dark Woman, Jon Antilles was a product of her rigorous and unconventionally harsh training methods, which were nonetheless effective. Among his many [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]], Antilles was capable of [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force flight]], which he demonstrated when he and the Jedi strike team jumped from a cliff and landed safely onto the moving Separatist island base during the mission to Queyta. Antilles was also an experienced swordsman, able to hold his own against Asajj Ventress, who was known to have slain several Jedi.<ref name="Jon" />

==Equipment==
Antilles owned a lightsaber that emitted a [[Color/Legends|green]] blade.<ref name="Jon" /> He also wore a long flowing [[Robe/Legends|robe]].<ref name="DB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jon Antilles first appeared in flashbacks in the the [[Republic 53|fifty-third issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series, written by [[W. Haden Blackman|Haden Blackman]], drawn by [[Brian Ching]] and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="Jon" /> on [[April 30]], [[2003]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54501/star_wars_republic_2002_53|text=Star Wars: Republic (2002) #53}}</ref> Antilles is a very common [[in-universe]] family name in ''[[Star Wars]]''.<ref name="dbwedge">{{DB|character|wedgeantilles|Antilles, Wedge}}</ref> [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], director of content management at [[Lucasfilm]], stated that Antilles is equivalent to "Jones" or "Smith". "Jon Antilles" is thus synonymous with the name [[Wikipedia:John Smith (name)|John Smith]], a generic alias used in Western culture.<ref name="SMITH">{{WebCite|url=http://www.galacticempire.net/swfaq.php#2a|text=GEN Star Wars FAQ|work=Galactic Empire Network|archivedate=20100706065240}}</ref>
